title: Mod Idea Designer: Create and List Mod Concepts

requirements.text: Implement backend data storage and APIs for mod ideas: allow creating a mod idea and listing all created mod ideas (persisted in canister stable state). Each mod idea must include: id, title, description, game/platform, tags (list of strings), createdAt timestamp, and author principal (if available).
acceptanceCriteria: A new mod idea can be created via a canister method and is persisted across canister upgrades/restarts via stable state.
A canister method returns the list of mod ideas in reverse-chronological order (newest first).
Each saved mod idea includes id, title, description, game/platform, tags, createdAt, and author principal (when the caller is known).
Backend rejects invalid payloads (e.g., empty title or empty description) with a clear error result.

requirements.text: Create a frontend page that lets users design a mod idea using a form with fields: Title, Description, Game/Platform, Tags (comma-separated input that becomes a list). On submit, save the idea via the backend and show success/error feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: The form prevents submission when required fields (Title, Description) are empty and shows inline validation messages in English.
Submitting a valid form calls the backend create method and resets the form on success.
The UI shows a clear error message when the backend returns an error or the call fails.

requirements.text: Create a frontend list view that displays all created mod ideas with basic filtering and viewing: show Title, Game/Platform, Tags, Created date, and a truncated Description preview; allow expanding/collapsing each item to read the full description.
acceptanceCriteria: On page load, the app fetches mod ideas from the backend and renders them newest-first.
Each mod idea card shows Title, Game/Platform, Tags, and Created date; description is truncated by default with a user control to expand/collapse.
If there are no mod ideas, the UI shows an empty state message in English (e.g., "No mod ideas yet").
The list has a simple client-side filter by Game/Platform text and/or tag text (case-insensitive).

requirements.text: Use React Query for fetching and mutations for mod ideas, including cache invalidation after creating a new idea so the list updates immediately without a manual refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: The list query is implemented with React Query and shows loading and error states in English.
The create mutation invalidates/refetches the mod idea list query on success.
No direct fetch logic is duplicated outside React Query for these operations.

requirements.text: Apply a coherent, creative visual theme across the app (colors, typography, spacing, and components) suitable for a "mod workshop" concept, avoiding default/plain styling. Use Tailwind + shadcn composition and keep the theme consistent across form, list, and empty/loading states.
acceptanceCriteria: All primary UI surfaces (header, form, list/cards, empty/loading/error states) share a consistent theme (palette, type scale, spacing).
Theme does not rely on blue/purple as the dominant palette unless explicitly requested later.
Uses existing shadcn UI components by composition (no edits to immutable UI component sources).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Do not edit any frontend files under immutablePaths (e.g., frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x), frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui).
User-facing text in the app must be in English.
nonGoals: Do not implement real-time collaboration or live updates (no websockets).
Do not add third-party authentication providers; only Internet Identity is allowed if authentication is later needed.
Do not implement external database integrations or off-chain storage.
Do not build complex moderation/rating/commenting features unless explicitly requested.
